change_comments.message: some moves needed and because of that I didn't review the testware
probably need to discuss some of the comments below
just had a quick look;seems like a good solution to me

can be merged after the bug fixes are merged.
-1 just to block from accidental merging.

inline_comments.message:
Nb. I _think_ this could be done generically in the test setup(), something like

    mockAlternateIdMatcher.existingCmHandleId(_) >> true

Nb. This could be refactored to simply call the new method:

    registerCmHandleWithAlternateId(dmiPlugin, cmHandleId, moduleSetTag, NO_ALTERNATE_ID)
remove these (content body not needed for GET request, and auth not needed)
should this be renamed if in line 109 is checking if its still cmhandle id or alternateId?
I think keeping it as cmHandle is okay as we're getting the cmhandleId even with an alternate Id being passed in. I could update the javadoc to reflect alternateIds being passed in as well.
yes would be helpful, could be assumed that its ONLY cmhandleId

suggestion 
if ...
cmHandleId = alternateIdMatcher.getCmHandleIdFromAlternateId(cmHandle)
else 
....

or change name of method as it actually returns a cmHandleId
i think this should be as 'and' for one of the given to mock the return
hmm doesnt seem to directly relate to the class
class is already alternateIdMatcher and paramter is alternateId , so i think this can be just getCmHandle

i can see the issue but stil it is NOT a cm =handle object maybe use the term 'cmHandleRefrence' to cover both possibilities?
'cmHandneIdOrAlternateId' is also an option
if you depend on the 'true' output you dont need to check invocation as well.
cmHandleId (or is it both, see previous comments in that case)

i woudl prefer an overloaded implementation of the method so you dont need to use 'NO_ALTERNATD_ID' in many other places...
I teh next 3 files woudl not need updates at all then

This seems to conflict with our new package guidelines.
Maybe this alternateId logic should be moved to service layer?
I wonder if it would make more sense to move this into the networkCmProxyFacade, so alternateId also works at service layer.
yes i think this logic should be in the service layer
probably should be n a util type class/package

why iterator and try to get next one. Is it a collection? then just use

return getCmHandleDataNodeByCmHandleId(cmHandleId).size() > 0
Alternate ID in pass-through data operations should return OK status
given: 'the URL for the pass-through data request'

why did you get rid of resourceAddress. I would still like to use it and change its' CmHandleId properties to 'reference' too
The resourceAddress was introduced to reduce the number of parameters (that could easily be mixed up). I think SonarQube wil complain when you have 8 or more parameter for a method...

as before, I still would like to use (a modified) CmResourceAddress
(you could maybe even implement the lookup in a common method for that class...)
in general the use of ! should be avoided if there is an easy solution without using ! in this case use size()>0 and then you don't need it...

bit worried about getting the whole object and just using the id (and possibly soon thereafter going back to get the object again)
I dont see an easy solution, it would need a dedicated methog(or flag) a few levels deep. But lets keep it in mind there might be performance consequences.
Then again we should pre-optimize if we are not aware of a problem. Instead we should pay close attention to existing performance test or maybe even write a performance test for around this usecase...  ie. "retrieve 1,000 cm handles by 'reference' and test with 1000 existing Id's v. 1,000 existing alternat ids

you are not asserting, just (correctly) setting up mock behavior
this should be done in a  given block before the when
given: 'the inventory persistence service returns a cm handle (or not)'

dont resvvle NOW only resolve when you actualy need the id...
suggestion:
change CmResourceADress.cmHandleId to cmHandleReference
cahnge from record to (normal) beamn so you can insert service 
add method like resolveCmHandleId() taht retruns teh id...
NB: need to check if feasible to also change in the openapi specs.
